stepfunctions.statemachine.Definition.Docs: The Amazon States Language definition of the state machine. The state machine definition must be in JSON or YAML, and the format of the object must match the format of your AWS Step Functions template file. See [Amazon States Language](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/step-functions/latest/dg/concepts-amazon-states-language.html).

stepfunctions.statemachine.DefinitionS3L.Docs: The name of the S3 bucket where the state machine definition is stored. The state machine definition must be a JSON or YAML file.

stepfunctions.statemachine.DefinitionStr.Docs: The Amazon States Language definition of the state machine. The state machine definition must be in JSON. See [Amazon States Language](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/step-functions/latest/dg/concepts-amazon-states-language.html).

stepfunctions.statemachine.DefinitionSub.Docs: A map (string to string) that specifies the mappings for placeholder variables in the state machine definition. This enables the customer to inject values obtained at runtime, for example from intrinsic functions, in the state machine definition. Variables can be template parameter names, resource logical IDs, resource attributes, or a variable in a key-value map.

stepfunctions.statemachine.LoggingConfig.Docs: Defines what execution history events are logged and where they are logged.  By default, the `level` is set to `OFF`. For more information see [Log Levels](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/step-functions/latest/dg/cloudwatch-log-level.html) in the AWS Step Functions User Guide.

stepfunctions.statemachine.RoleArn.Docs: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role to use for this state machine.

stepfunctions.statemachine.StateMachineN.Docs: The name of the state machine.   A name must *not* contain:  + white space+ brackets ` { } [ ]` + wildcard characters `? *` + special characters `"
